income_thresholds.description: BPL / NFSA families: completely free treatment (no co-pay). No income ceiling for free tier — BPL status is the qualifier.
income_thresholds.source: Karnataka State Government / Suvarna Arogya Suraksha Trust

income_thresholds.description: APL (Above Poverty Line) families: eligible with 30% co-payment by patient. No upper income limit for APL tier, but co-pay applies.
income_thresholds.source: Karnataka State Government / Suvarna Arogya Suraksha Trust

exclusion_rules.description: Only residents of Karnataka are eligible for Arogya Karnataka
Non-BPL/non-NFSA families are still eligible but must pay 30% co-pay for treatment. This is a soft exclusion — they get coverage but with cost-sharing.

family_criteria.family_definition: Family as per ration card or BPL list. All members are covered. Coverage is floater across all family members. Both BPL and APL families can enroll.

covered_procedures: Hospitalization at government and empanelled private hospitals
Orthopedic (joint replacement, fracture surgery, spine surgery)

required_documents.name: BPL Card / NFSA Ration Card (for free tier)
required_documents.notes: Required for free (zero co-pay) tier. Without BPL/NFSA proof, you are enrolled in the 30% co-pay APL tier.

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: Primary identity document for all family members. Required for e-KYC at enrollment and hospital admission.

required_documents.name: Ration Card (any type)
required_documents.notes: Used to identify family members for enrollment. BPL ration card also serves as eligibility proof for free tier.

required_documents.name: Address Proof (Karnataka residence)
required_documents.notes: Must prove current residence in Karnataka

required_documents.name: Mobile Number
required_documents.notes: For OTP verification and claim status communications

enrollment_steps: Step 1: Visit any government hospital or empanelled private hospital in Karnataka
Step 2: Go to the Arogya Mitra desk (available at major hospitals)
Step 3: Submit Aadhaar card and ration card (BPL/NFSA for free tier, any for APL tier)
Step 4: Complete Aadhaar e-KYC (biometric or OTP verification)
Step 5: System verifies BPL/NFSA status against state database for tier determination
Step 6: Family members' details are entered and verified
Step 7: Arogya Karnataka health card is issued (BPL: free tier, APL: 30% co-pay tier)
Step 8: For BPL families: cashless treatment up to Rs 5 lakh. For APL families: treatment with 30% co-pay up to Rs 5 lakh.
description_for_embedding: Arogya Karnataka is Karnataka's state health insurance scheme providing Rs 5 lakh per family per year. It has a two-tier structure: BPL/NFSA families receive completely free cashless treatment, while APL (above poverty line) families can access the same treatment with a 30% co-payment. This makes it semi-universal — all Karnataka residents are eligible, but cost-sharing differs by economic status. Covers hospitalization across government and empanelled private hospitals. Managed by the Suvarna Arogya Suraksha Trust. Enrollment via government hospitals and Arogya Mitra desks. Helpline 104.
